name: Puccia (Olive Studded Rolls)
recipeIngredient: 4 1/3 oz Small, meaty black olives (pitted) in water or brine
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the yeast into the warm water in a large bowl or heavy mixer. Let proof for 10 minutes.
2. Add the remaining water and the biga, and mix until well blended.
3. Add the flours and mix until the dough comes together (about 1 to 2 minutes). The dough will not come away from the sides of the bowl.
4. With very wet hands, knead until the dough is very silky, soft, and elastic. Knead on a lightly floured board to eliminate stickiness.
5. Knead in the olives by hand to ensure they remain whole. Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl and let rise, covered, until tripled (3 to 4 hours).
6. Pour the wet dough out of the bowl onto a floured work surface. Do not punch it down.
7. Flour your hands and flatten each piece. Roll them up lengthwise.
8. Turn the dough 90 degrees and roll them up again.
9. Shape each piece into a ball. Set on a floured baking sheet.
10. Toss a hailstorm of flour over the tops and cover with a heavy cloth. Let rise until doubled (1 to 1.5 hours).
11. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
12. Bake the rolls until they are crusty and golden, about 25 to 30 minutes.
VARIATIONS:
- Puccia di Grano Duro: Substitute 7 1/2 cups durum flour for the flours above. Knead the dough for an extra two minutes.
- Puccia di Uva Passa: Use raisins (softened in water for 30 minutes) instead of olives.
- Pizzi: Add 1/4 to 3/8 teaspoon hot pepper flakes to the vegetables of Cucuzzara and fold them into the dough in place of the olives.
